mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and a contraction in retail sales indicates a synchronized collapse in residential and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months and RSAFS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint decline of HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months where INDPRO rises during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 205 indicate persistent price pressures that force markets to price in tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive PAYEMS growth over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP coinciding with declining retail sales confirms a broad demand shock that compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and manufacturing demand, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months where INDPRO accelerates during mid-cycle expansions.
